pubmed_links: [Shao & Vawter (2008) Shared gene expression alterations in schizophrenia and bipolar disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18191109/); [Masana et al. (2014) The stress-inducible actin-interacting protein DRR1 shapes social behavior](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24998413/); [Kretzschmar et al. (2018) The stress-inducible protein DRR1 exerts distinct effects on actin dynamics](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30545002/)
summary: FAM107A/DRR1 is a stress-inducible actin-binding protein highly expressed in the nervous system whose expression is altered in the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex of individuals with schizophrenia and bipolar disorder; in animal models it regulates actin dynamics, dendritic spine formation, synaptic function, and social behavior, suggesting a role in stress-related psychiatric resilience. (emerging evidence)
